_comment: Lockout/Tagout (LOTO) and PPE procedures keyed by hazard type. Modeled on OSHA 29 CFR 1910.147 (The Control of Hazardous Energy) and 1910.333. Illustrative for demo use; a real deployment binds to the facility's energy-control procedure.

ppe: Arc-rated face shield + balaclava (per incident energy)
Class 0 insulating gloves with leather protectors
loto_steps: Notify all affected employees that a lockout is required and why.
Identify ALL energy sources for the asset (main disconnect, control transformer, capacitor banks).
Shut down the equipment using its normal stopping procedure.
Isolate energy: open the disconnect switch / unplug at source.
Apply your assigned lock and a durable tag to the disconnect in the OFF position.
Release/verify zero stored energy (discharge capacitors, bleed VFD DC bus).
VERIFY isolation: attempt a normal start (try-out), then meter the terminals to confirm 0V before contact.
Perform the work.
Reverse: clear tools, confirm personnel clear, remove lock/tag, restore energy, notify affected employees.

mechanical.hazard: Hazardous mechanical / rotating energy (pinch, entanglement, crush)
ppe: No loose clothing, jewelry, or untied hair near rotating parts
loto_steps: Notify affected employees of the planned lockout.
Stop the conveyor using the normal stop, then the local disconnect.
Apply lock and tag to the motor disconnect in the OFF position.
Block or restrain any parts that could move by gravity (incline sections, take-up counterweights).
Relieve stored mechanical energy (release belt tension at the take-up if servicing the belt).
VERIFY: attempt a normal start to confirm the drive will not energize.
Perform the work.
Reverse the sequence; confirm guards are reinstalled before restoring power.

ppe: Class ABC fire extinguisher staged within reach
loto_steps: If smoke or burning smell is present, hit the E-stop immediately and evacuate the immediate area if active fire is suspected.
Do NOT touch motor housings, bearings, or brakes until verified cool or measured with a non-contact IR thermometer.
De-energize and apply lockout per the electrical procedure.
Allow components to cool to a safe handling temperature (< 120 F / 49 C).
Inspect for the heat source (seized bearing, dragging brake, overloaded motor) before restart.
Clear and document before restoring power.
Notify affected employees.
Stop equipment via normal stop.
Isolate and lock/tag the primary energy source.
Verify zero energy state before contact.
Perform work; restore and notify when complete.
